TL;DR

• The strongest rebuild partners pair Next.js with a headless CMS such as Sanity, Storyblok, Contentful, or Payload, and deploy on Vercel with incremental static regeneration for speed that survives frequent content changes.

• Clutch scores cluster tightly among the leading specialists, so the real signal is documented migration work with named clients, not the rating alone.

• Fit depends on project shape: a content-site rebuild, a commerce replatform, a net-new product, and an embedded engineering need all point to different firms.
What does rebuilding a website on a modern tech stack actually mean?
Most rebuilds start from an existing site on WordPress, Drupal, Pimcore, or a page builder that has become slow to load and slow to change. The goal is rarely a fresh design for its own sake. It is usually the same content and URLs, faster, on infrastructure a non-technical team can operate on.
On a modern stack that means a decoupled frontend in Next.js, a headless CMS for the content layer, and deployment on a platform like Vercel so pages stay fast without going stale. Speed of delivery then comes down to migration discipline (moving content and preserving SEO without a traffic drop), a reusable component library so editors get a block editor instead of a developer queue, and senior judgment about scope.
How do you evaluate an agency for a fast website rebuild?
Three things are worth checking before the design conversation starts, and they apply to every agency below equally. First, Clutch standing read for consistency across many reviews rather than a single score. Second, a named migration case study (WordPress, Drupal, Pimcore, or Webflow to headless) with a described outcome, not just a claimed capability. Third, whether the engagement model and minimum budget match a team of roughly 50 to 1,000 people. The profiles that follow describe what each firm does and is known for; the shortlisting section at the end shows how to weigh them against your own project.
Which agencies are best for a fast website rebuild in 2026?
The strongest options in 2026 are specialist Next.js and headless CMS studios, led by Naturaily, Pagepro, Bejamas, Blazity, and FocusReactive, with larger firms like DEPT and Netguru fitting enterprise-scale programs.
1. Naturaily — headless rebuilds for content-driven teams
Based in: Wrocław (Poland)
As a Next.js and headless development company, Naturaily rebuilds content-driven websites and commerce platforms that load fast, convert, and stay easy for marketing teams to maintain. They work across Sanity, Storyblok, Contentful, and Payload rather than a single CMS, so the content model fits the project instead of the other way round.
Recent work includes moving Capitalise.com off Pimcore to a headless setup on Next.js and Storyblok, with A/B testing built in so the marketing team can experiment without developers, and migrating Expert Institute from WordPress to Next.js and Sanity to improve Core Web Vitals and organic traffic while making the site editable by a non-technical team. Both engagements are rated 5.0 on the company’s 4.9/5 Clutch profile.
2. Pagepro — Next.js and Sanity specialist studio
Based in: Białystok (Poland), London (UK)
Pagepro builds marketing sites and web applications on Next.js and modern React, with headless CMS implementation, performance work, and long-term support. They are an Official Sanity and Vercel Partner and rank first globally on Clutch for Sanity development.
For migration clients they built Nexity, a production-ready Next.js and Sanity starter drawn from dozens of migrations, with preconfigured SEO, live preview, revalidation, redirects, and structured content modeling that shortens setup and reduces risk.
3. Bejamas — performance-first composable web
Based in: Remote (Wrocław roots)
Bejamas is a tech-agnostic team focused on speed: Core Web Vitals, edge delivery, and decoupled architecture across Next.js, Astro, Gatsby, and several CMS platforms, with more than 200 projects delivered.
Their portfolio includes a Drupal-to-headless migration for Finanzchef24 that moved hundreds of pages and brought the homepage to passing Core Web Vitals, plus work for Mambu, Armorblox, and Backlinko. They are known for giving an honest opinion on whether a given stack is the right call before building.
4. Blazity — scalable Next.js platforms
Based in: Warsaw (Poland)
Blazity builds marketing sites, web applications, and commerce platforms with an emphasis on performance and maintainable architecture, plus headless CMS migration and technical audits. They are a Vercel Silver Partner and placed on Deloitte’s Technology Fast 50 Central Europe in 2024.
They maintain a widely used open-source enterprise Next.js commerce starter and report outcomes like a large Core Web Vitals improvement for CookUnity and a 600-plus page WordPress-to-headless migration.
5. FocusReactive — vendor-neutral headless migrations
Based in: London (UK), Amsterdam (NL), Warsaw (Poland)
FocusReactive is an engineering-led headless CMS agency that holds official partnerships with four platforms at once: Sanity, Storyblok, Contentful, and Payload. That breadth means platform recommendations are less likely to be shaped by what the team already knows.
Migration is the core competency, including WordPress and Webflow moves to Next.js plus a headless CMS, and an AI-assisted pipeline for large content sets. One Storyblok build reported a 97 Lighthouse score and organic traffic gains after launch.
6. Pixelmatters — design and engineering under one roof
Based in: Porto (Portugal)
Pixelmatters builds high-performance websites and digital products for B2B and enterprise clients, pairing design with Next.js engineering. Reported work includes a SaaS redesign with a notable performance gain and a Next.js migration for an e-learning provider that improved load times.
The team suits growth-stage SaaS companies that want a single partner for both design and build rather than coordinating two vendors.
7. Netguru — design-led digital product partner
Based in: Poznań (Poland)
Netguru is a large, long-established firm where product design is a genuine core competency, with deep experience in fintech and consumer products for well-known European brands. They bring team scale and design depth to rebuilds that sit inside a broader transformation effort.
Their strength is product and design rather than narrow specialist frontend engineering, and pricing runs higher than smaller regional peers.
8. Brainhub — senior engineers for existing teams
Based in: Gliwice (Poland)
Brainhub is a software engineering agency built around team extension: embedding senior React and Next.js engineers into a client’s existing organization rather than delivering a turnkey build. They also work with Node.js, .NET, cloud infrastructure, and headless CMS platforms such as Contentful.
The model fits companies that already have engineering leadership and need frontend depth for a defined period, with recognition from Deloitte and the Financial Times.
9. DEPT® — enterprise digital transformation
Based in: Amsterdam (Netherlands), global
DEPT operates across 30-plus locations as a B Corp and Climate Neutral certified agency, handling large multi-stakeholder programs where a rebuild is one workstream among many. Minimums start around $100,000 with hourly rates in the $150 to $199 range.
The scale suits enterprise budgets and governance. On complex engagements some clients note delays and communication gaps, which is common at this size.
10. Merixstudio — full-stack studio with integrated design
Based in: Poznań (Poland)
Founded in 1999, Merixstudio pairs full-stack engineering with a strong design practice and carries one of the higher review volumes in this group, at 4.9 across nearly a hundred Clutch reviews. Their heritage spans Python, Django, React, and Vue, with clients in fintech, healthtech, and enterprise SaaS.
They fit larger builds that combine a content site with custom application work. A few reviews note that early estimates can run optimistic.
11. Boldare — product development and iteration
Based in: Gliwice (Poland)
Boldare is a product-development partner with an agile, embedded delivery style on React, Node, and TypeScript, running discovery and iterative build rather than fixed-scope site delivery.
The approach suits teams that want a partner shaping the product as it goes. Some reviews point to mobile development as a relatively weaker area.
12. Software Mansion — deep frontend engineering
Based in: Kraków (Poland)
Software Mansion is known globally for React and React Native expertise and is a significant open-source contributor in that ecosystem, increasingly applying that depth to complex Next.js web applications.
They fit technically demanding builds where frontend engineering, not design or content, is the hardest part of the project.
13. Big Bite — headless WordPress and Next.js
Based in: United Kingdom
Big Bite is unusual on this list for straddling headless WordPress and Next.js. Their public work covers large publishers and editorial sites moving from a traditional WordPress frontend to a Next.js or Faust-powered headless one.
The team fits publishers and content-heavy organizations that want to keep WordPress as the editorial backend while modernizing the frontend.
14. The Frontend Company — frontend-led headless builds
Based in: Remote (global)
The Frontend Company pairs React and Next.js with a range of headless CMS platforms including Contentful, Sanity, Strapi, and Directus, emphasizing user-centric design and longer-term partnerships.
They suit teams that want a frontend-led partner and flexibility across CMS options. Public performance metrics on named clients are limited.
15. 10Clouds — agile web and product house
Based in: Warsaw (Poland)
Operating since 2009, 10Clouds delivers digital products and web applications across many industries, with Next.js services covering server-side rendering, static generation, and performance work, and a client roster that has included well-known names.
The delivery model emphasizes agile practices and end-to-end lifecycle management, fitting teams that want a broad product house rather than a narrow framework specialist.
Website rebuild agencies compared: Clutch ratings, stack, and focus

How do you choose the right rebuild agency for your project?
The order above reflects one job: a fast rebuild of a content-driven site on a modern stack for a growing team. Change the job and the order changes. For a net-new SaaS product, the product-led firms (Netguru, Boldare, Pixelmatters) move up. To embed engineers in an existing team, Brainhub leads. For enterprise scale and governance, DEPT is the anchor. To keep WordPress as an editorial backend, Big Bite is the specific answer.
Three questions separate the specialists more usefully than any score:

• Which CMS, and does the agency hold that partnership? A firm partnered with one CMS tends to recommend it. FocusReactive’s four-way partnership and Naturaily’s multi-CMS delivery reduce that pull; Pagepro’s Sanity depth is an advantage if Sanity is already your choice.

• What is the documented migration outcome? Ask for a named case study with before-and-after Core Web Vitals or traffic. Several strong firms here publish little, so this is where a shortlist gets real.

• Does the engagement model fit the budget? Minimums run from around $10,000 (Blazity and Bejamas entry work) to $25,000-plus (Pagepro, Naturaily larger builds) to $100,000-plus (DEPT).
The most reliable signal is whether an agency’s three most recent projects look like the one you are about to start.
Common questions about modern website rebuilds
How long does a modern website rebuild take?
A light rebuild on an existing content model can ship in a few weeks. A full replatforming with content migration, a new component library, and integrations typica
